pattern: For each word, the shortest dictionary root that prefixes it wins, which a trie finds by stopping at the first root marker walking down the word.
hints: Process the sentence word by word; the answer for a word depends only on whether some root is a prefix of it.
Among all roots that are prefixes of a word, you want the shortest, so search prefixes from short to long.

Build a trie of the roots, marking word-ends. For each word, walk the trie following the word's characters; the first end-marker you hit gives the shortest matching root, which replaces the word. If none, keep the word.

edge_inputs.description: several words replaced by their roots
input: the cattle was rattled by the battery

edge_inputs.description: shortest root must win when two roots both prefix a word
single word, single root that is a prefix
root longer than word cannot match

stress.description: random roots and a sentence of random words, some derived from roots
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alpha = 'abcde'
    nd = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 60)))
    roots = []
    for _ in range(nd):
        ln = rng.randrange(1, 6)
        roots.append(''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(ln)))
    nw = rng.randrange(1, max(2, min(size, 60)))
    words = []
    for _ in range(nw):
        if roots and rng.random() < 0.5:
            base = rng.choice(roots)
            extra = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(rng.randrange(0, 4)))
            words.append(base + extra)
        else:
            ln = rng.randrange(1, 7)
            words.append(''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(ln)))
    sentence = ' '.join(words)
    return [roots, sentence]
stress.note: half of words are derived from a root so replacements occur
